query: I generated 50k synthetic patient records using CTGAN, fit on a real 50k-record dataset. I split the real data into 40k train (used to fit CTGAN) and 10k test (held out). I want to share the synth set with a research partner so they can train a binary readmission classifier without ever seeing real PHI. How do I audit whether the synth set is good enough that their model will still perform on real patients?
expected_behavior: Names the TSTR (Train-on-Synthetic, Test-on-Real) protocol as the primary utility metric and computes the utility ratio as TSTR / TRTR (not on synth alone)
Requires fitting at least two downstream model families (linear / logistic baseline and a tree-based model like RandomForest) because a synth set can favor one family over another
Reports a 95% confidence interval on the utility ratio via bootstrap on the held-out real test set, not just a point estimate
